frameworks.name: representational geometry
frameworks.one_line: compare neural coding axes of perception and working memory across cortex

frameworks.name: high-dimensional flexible coding
frameworks.one_line: perception and memory occupy distinct linearly separable subspaces

frameworks.name: factorized representational format
frameworks.one_line: abstraction across perception and memory enables cross-decoding generalization

frameworks.name: cortical hierarchy of abstraction
frameworks.one_line: flexibility versus abstraction tradeoff varies from sensory to transmodal regions

core_question: Do visual perception and visual working memory share the same representational geometry across the cortical hierarchy?
key_assumptions: stimulus coding dimensions can be recovered with linear decoders from 7T fMRI
face vs scene category provides a coding axis comparable across states
perception and working memory states are dissociable within the same trial
manipulations: attended content (face vs scene) on a blended sample
match vs mismatch test for the attended aspect
perceived versus memorized content within trial
cortical region from early sensory to transmodal association
key_findings: stimulus coding dimension rotates when perceptual content enters working memory
exclusive perception-memory combinations are linearly decodable
robust cross-decoding across perception and memory indicates factorized formats
early sensory areas emphasize flexibility while transmodal areas show greater abstraction
interpretations: high-dimensional flexible coding may prevent confusion between perception and memory
balance of flexibility and abstraction varies along the cortical hierarchy
